name: requests
summary: HTTP for Humans — the most downloaded Python library

story.problem: Python's built-in urllib requires dozens of lines of boilerplate for a simple GET request: manually building URLs, encoding parameters, handling redirects, decoding responses, and managing connections. Every project reimplemented the same plumbing.
story.mental_model: Think of requests like a browser tab in code. You create a Session (the browser), make requests to URLs, and get back Response objects — status, headers, and body all in one place. You never touch raw sockets or HTTP headers unless you want to.
story.when_to_use: Anytime your Python code needs to call an HTTP API, download a file, submit a form, or scrape a page. It's the default choice for synchronous HTTP work.
story.when_not_to_use: If you need async/await support, use httpx instead. If you're making thousands of concurrent requests, consider aiohttp.

alternatives.name: httpx
alternatives.reason: Same API, but supports async/await and HTTP/2

alternatives.name: aiohttp
alternatives.reason: High-performance async HTTP, larger API surface

alternatives.name: urllib3
alternatives.reason: Lower-level; requests itself is built on top of it

nodes.label: requests
nodes.summary: Top-level module. Import this to start.

nodes.label: Session
nodes.summary: Persists cookies, auth, and headers across multiple requests. Use when talking to the same server repeatedly.

nodes.label: get()
nodes.summary: Send a GET request. The most common entry point.

nodes.label: post()
nodes.summary: Send a POST request with a body — JSON, form data, or file upload.

nodes.label: put()
nodes.summary: Replace a resource at the given URL.

nodes.label: delete()
nodes.summary: Delete a resource at the given URL.

nodes.label: Response
nodes.summary: Holds everything the server sent back: status code, headers, and body.

nodes.label: PreparedRequest
nodes.summary: A fully-built HTTP request before it's sent. Useful for inspection and custom signing.

nodes.label: HTTPAdapter
nodes.summary: Controls how requests are sent: retry logic, SSL, connection pooling.

nodes.label: RequestException
nodes.summary: Base class for all requests exceptions. Catch this to handle any network error.

nodes.label: Timeout
nodes.summary: Raised when the server doesn't respond within the timeout window.

nodes.label: ConnectionError
nodes.summary: Raised when no network connection is available or the server refuses.

steps.label: POST with json=
steps.explanation: Pass json= and requests sets Content-Type automatically.

steps.label: Reuse across requests
steps.explanation: The session reuses the TCP connection and sends your header on every call.

steps.label: Import
steps.code:
from requests.adapters import HTTPAdapter
from urllib3.util.retry import Retry

steps.label: Make request
steps.explanation: If the server returns 500/502/503, requests waits and retries up to 3 times automatically.
